scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces generated text or document outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within laboratory and research operations scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic generated text or document outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Mixture handling now documented in workflow step 4 with explicit logic: most hazardous component determines container. Mixture rule note added to Waste Categories table. All core use cases covered.

reliability.note: Mixture handling step added. Error handling documented; unknown chemical fallback well-designed; fabrication prohibition present.

performance_context.note: No references/ directory; waste category table is inline in SKILL.md; no progressive disclosure.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear with mixture handling step. Stress-case rules defined; feedback design good with five-block output structure.

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable for lab staff; forgiveness good — chemical name is the only required input.

security.note: No credentials required; input validation present; no sensitive data exposure risk.

maintainability.note: Clean structure; waste category table is inline — adding new categories still requires editing SKILL.md. Mixture rule now documented.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; no progressive disclosure; composability limited — no structured output schema for LIMS integration. Mixture handling improves functional coverage.

Output assigns the halogenated (orange) container for the mixed waste
Output emits a note about halogenated solvent precedence in mixed waste

assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.note: Confirmed: output suggests the closest category based on chemical as expected.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate a disposal category for the unknown chemical
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.note: Confirmed: output recommends ehs verification for unknown chemicals as expected.

key_strengths: Mixture handling now explicitly documented in workflow step 4 — most hazardous component determines container, with halogenated precedence rule
Mixture rule note added to Waste Categories table provides clear reference for mixed waste streams
Unknown chemical fallback (suggest closest category based on chemical class) is a well-designed graceful degradation path
Fabrication prohibition for unknown chemicals is a critical safety property for this domain

recommendations.title: Waste category database is inline — hard to update and extend
recommendations.problem: The seven waste categories and their accepted chemicals are defined inline in SKILL.md, making it difficult to add new chemicals or update categories without editing the core skill file.
recommendations.root_cause: No references/ or data/ directory was created for the waste category database.
recommendations.fix: Move the waste category definitions to a references/waste_categories.md or data/chemicals.json file and reference it from SKILL.md to enable updates without modifying the skill logic.

recommendations.title: No structured output schema for LIMS or EHS system integration
recommendations.problem: The skill outputs human-readable text but provides no structured JSON output option, limiting integration with laboratory information management systems (LIMS) or EHS platforms.
recommendations.root_cause: Output format is not parameterized; no --format flag or output schema is documented.
recommendations.fix: Add a --format flag (text/json) and document the JSON output schema in SKILL.md or references/ to enable downstream integration with LIMS and EHS systems.
